subject: English Language Arts
topic: Plan, Build, Grow — Day 1: English Stream Worksheet

teacher_guide.big_idea: English-stream students are using this project as their real-world communication context. Day 1 establishes vocabulary and gets them reading a functional document — the client brief — for the first time.
learning_goals: Identify the key information in a client brief: who, what, where.
Use at least two project vocabulary words accurately.
Write or dictate one question for the client.
materials: Client brief (read aloud by teacher first)
This worksheet — one per English-stream student
Vocabulary word wall or sentence frame strip

timing.activity: Students work through this worksheet independently or with EA support. Teacher circulates.

teacher_notes: Read the vocabulary anchor aloud before students begin any questions.
Q1–Q2 can be answered orally and scribed — accept any response form.
Q4 (the question for the client) is the most important English outcome today.
Students who finish early can write a second question or try a full sentence using two vocabulary words.

anchor: client — the person who gave us the job
planter box — a container for growing plants
materials — what we use to build it
dimensions — the size: length, width, height
report — telling the client what we did
worksheet.tip: You can write, draw, circle, or ask someone to scribe. All three count.

questions.q_text:
Who is the client? What do they want?

The client is: ___________________
They want us to: ___________________
It will go: ___________________

Circle the words that describe what you heard in the client brief:

big   small   wooden   metal   outdoor   indoor   simple   fancy   urgent   flexible

Now circle TWO vocabulary words from the anchor above that connect to what the client wants.

What did you notice about the space when you saw it?

Sentence frame: I noticed that the space is ___. This means we will probably need to ___.

Write ONE question you would ask the client before the team starts building.

Question starter ideas: How big should...   Can you tell us...   What do you want planted...

My question: ___________________

Commitment card — what will YOU do on this project? Circle one:

PLAN the design     WRITE the reports     RECORD the growth     PRESENT to the client

In one sentence (or out loud): I am going to ___________________

self_check: I know who the client is and what they want.
I used at least one vocabulary word from the anchor.
I wrote or said one question for the client.

exit_ticket.title: Day 1 Exit Check — English Stream
exit_ticket.prompt:
Write or say one sentence that uses TWO vocabulary words from today.

Example: The client needs a planter box with the right dimensions for the space.

Your sentence:
success_criteria: Student used two vocabulary words correctly.
